title: Static vs. Interactive vs. Adaptive Textbooks

zones.label: Static — Level 1
zones.summary: Fixed PDF or print content — no interactivity
facts: Text and static images only — no dynamic elements
No learner interaction — same content for every reader
Typically PDF files, printed books, or basic HTML pages
Difficulty: cannot adapt to the learner's pace or prior knowledge
Assessment: external only (paper tests, separate LMS)

zones.label: Interactive — Level 2
zones.summary: Hyperlinked MkDocs site with embedded MicroSims and inline quizzes
facts: Hyperlinked navigation across chapters and glossary
Interactive MicroSims using p5.js, Chart.js, vis-network
Built with MkDocs Material theme, deployed to GitHub Pages
Learning graph drives concept ordering and dependency tracking

zones.label: Adaptive — Level 3+
zones.summary: AI-personalized content that adapts to each individual learner
facts: xAPI (Experience API) logs every learner interaction
AI generates on-demand explanations at the learner's level
Difficulty and pacing adapt based on quiz performance
Knowledge graph ensures prerequisites are mastered first

quiz.question: Which textbook level uses xAPI to track learner progress and adapts content difficulty based on quiz performance?
quiz.explanation: Level 3+ adaptive textbooks log every learner interaction via xAPI and use that data to adjust difficulty, pacing, and even generate personalized explanations on demand.

quiz.question: Which textbook level is built with MkDocs Material, embeds p5.js MicroSims, and includes inline quizzes — but does NOT yet personalize content to the individual learner?
quiz.explanation: Level 2 interactive textbooks are the target of the claude-skills workflow: MkDocs Material + MicroSims + quizzes, deployed to GitHub Pages, with a learning-graph-driven concept order.

quiz.question: Which textbook level delivers the same fixed content to every reader and requires external tools (paper tests, an LMS) for assessment?
quiz.explanation: Level 1 static textbooks are PDFs or basic HTML with no embedded interactivity. Assessment must happen outside the book (in a separate LMS or on paper) because the content itself cannot respond to the learner.
